database_purpose: AI-ready FieldViewer structured metadata for safe Text-to-SQL over wells, layers, grids, tops, and production summaries.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT well_name, x_utm, y_utm FROM v_well_locations ORDER BY well_name
example_questions.question: List all wells with coordinates.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT reservoir, COUNT(*) AS well_count FROM v_well_locations GROUP BY reservoir ORDER BY well_count DESC
example_questions.question: Show wells by reservoir.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT well_name, status FROM v_well_locations WHERE reservoir IS NULL OR reservoir = '' ORDER BY well_name
example_questions.question: Find wells without reservoir assigned.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT layer_name, layer_type, file_path, enabled FROM v_layer_catalog ORDER BY layer_name
example_questions.explanation: Reads the safe layer catalog view.
example_questions.question: List available map layers.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT grid_name, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax, zmin, zmax, unit FROM v_grid_catalog ORDER BY grid_name
example_questions.question: List available grids and their extents.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT well_name, date, oil_rate, gas_rate, water_rate, water_cut, pressure FROM v_production_by_well WHERE well_name = 'WELL_NAME' ORDER BY date
example_questions.explanation: Replace WELL_NAME with a real well name before execution.
example_questions.question: Show production records for a selected well.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT well_name, MAX(date) AS latest_production_date FROM v_production_by_well GROUP BY well_name ORDER BY well_name
example_questions.explanation: Aggregates latest available production record per well.
example_questions.question: Show latest production date per well if data exists.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT well_name, formation, tvdss, twt, source FROM v_well_tops WHERE well_name = 'WELL_NAME' ORDER BY formation
example_questions.explanation: Replace WELL_NAME with a real well name before execution.
example_questions.question: List tops for a selected well.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT well_name, formation, tvdss FROM v_well_tops WHERE formation LIKE '%PL6%' ORDER BY well_name
example_questions.question: Find wells with PL6 tops if available.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS well_count FROM v_well_locations GROUP BY status ORDER BY well_count DESC
example_questions.question: Count wells by status.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT wellname, date, daily_oil_bbl, daily_water_bbl, daily_gas_mscf, pressure_psi FROM v_production_full WHERE wellname = 'WELL_NAME' ORDER BY date
example_questions.explanation: Replace WELL_NAME with a real well name before execution.
example_questions.question: Show full production records for a selected well.

example_questions.expected_sql: SELECT res_name, MAX(oil_cum_bbl) AS max_oil_cum_bbl, MAX(water_cum_bbl) AS max_water_cum_bbl FROM v_production_full GROUP BY res_name ORDER BY max_oil_cum_bbl DESC
example_questions.explanation: Uses the complete production records rather than the compact summary table.
example_questions.question: Show cumulative oil and water by reservoir from the full production table.

relationships.description: Each instance belongs to one project.
Each well belongs to one FieldViewer instance.
Formation tops are joined to wells by well_id.
Production records are joined to wells by well_id.
Layers belong to a FieldViewer instance.
Grids belong to a FieldViewer instance.

safe_query_rules: Only SELECT and WITH statements are allowed.
Multiple statements and semicolon-separated SQL are rejected.
Write, DDL, attachment, PRAGMA, vacuum, and execution keywords are blocked.
Queries receive a default LIMIT when missing and cannot exceed the configured maximum.
Tables marked is_ai_visible=0 are hidden from AI-generated SQL.
The LLM must validate SQL before execution and must never access SQLite directly.

tables.business_purpose: Tracks prior AI-assisted questions and generated summaries.
tables.description: Historical AI query records and summaries.
tables.grain: one row per AI interaction record

tables.business_purpose: Stores lightweight interpretation notes without changing source geoscience files.
tables.description: User or workflow annotations linked to wells or coordinates.

tables.business_purpose: Supports discovery of available grids and their spatial/depth ranges.

columns.description: Local folder or file reference for the packaged grid.
columns.name: file_path

columns.description: Packaged grid name shown in FieldViewer.
columns.name: grid_name

tables.description: Catalog of packaged grids and their extents.
tables.grain: one row per packaged grid per instance

tables.business_purpose: Connects project-level data to a specific generated app instance.
tables.description: Generated FieldViewer app instances and their local data/app roots.

tables.business_purpose: Explains which spatial layers are available for display or analysis.

columns.description: Local file path for the source layer metadata or payload.
columns.name: file_path

columns.description: Layer category such as grid source, wells, stations, or polygon.
columns.name: layer_type

tables.description: Catalog of map and subsurface layers available to an instance.
tables.grain: one row per layer per instance

tables.business_purpose: Supports detailed Text-to-SQL questions over the complete production history, including rates, monthly volumes, cumulative volumes, injection, status, reservoir, and well metadata.

columns.description: Average daily oil production from source daily-average column.
columns.name: daily_oil_bbl

columns.description: Gas injection volume from the source table.
columns.name: gas_injection_mscf

columns.description: Monthly oil volume from the source production table.
columns.name: monthly_oil_bbl

columns.description: Pressure measurement from the production source when available.
columns.name: pressure_psi

columns.description: Reservoir or zone name from the production source table.
columns.name: res_name

columns.description: UWI or well identifier from the production source table.
columns.name: uwi

columns.description: Water injection volume from the source table.
columns.name: water_injection_bbl

columns.description: Well name from the production source table.
columns.name: wellname

tables.description: Full imported production table with all source production rows and normalized column names.
tables.grain: one row per source production record per well/date/vector

tables.business_purpose: Supports production rate, water cut, pressure, and latest-date questions.

columns.description: Pressure measurement when present in the source.
columns.name: pressure

tables.description: Normalized production measurements by well and date.
tables.grain: one row per well per production date

tables.business_purpose: Identifies the field or project that owns generated instances.
tables.description: FieldViewer project records and engineering CRS context.

tables.business_purpose: Preferred AI view for production questions.

columns.description: Well name from the full production source table.
